examples.title: Dijkstra's SPF Algorithm for OSPF
examples.description: Implementation of SPF algorithm used by OSPF to calculate shortest path
examples.explanation: The SPF algorithm maintains a shortest path tree starting from the local router. It uses a priority queue to always process the lowest-cost node first. When network topology changes (LSA received), SPF recalculates affected paths. OSPF optimizes this by maintaining the tree and only updating changed portions.
examples.timeComplexity: O(L + E log V) where L=LSAs, E=edges, V=vertices

examples.title: OSPF Hello Protocol
examples.explanation: Hello packets establish and maintain neighbor relationships. Routers become neighbors when they see each other in their Hello packets (bidirectional communication). Dead timer (4× Hello) must expire before declaring neighbor down.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) per packet, O(n) for n neighbors

examples.title: OSPF LSA Flooding Mechanism
examples.description: How OSPF distributes link-state information reliably
examples.code:
// OSPF LSA Flooding

FLOOD_LSA(lsa, receiving_interface):
 // Create LSA instance with sequence number and age
 lsa.sequence_number = next_sequence()
 lsa.age = 0
 
 // Check if we have this LSA already
 IF lsa NOT IN lsdb THEN:
 ADD lsa to lsdb
 ADD lsa to retransmission_list
 
 // Flood to all neighbors except sender
 FOR each neighbor ON receiving_interface:
 IF neighbor.state >= 2-Way THEN:
 IF neighbor.is_dr OR neighbor.is_bdr OR NOT (link_type is broadcast) THEN:
 SEND_LSA_DIRECT(lsa, neighbor)
 ELSE:
 // DROTHERs only send to DR/BDR
 SEND_LSA_DIRECT(lsa, dr_router)
 SEND_LSA_DIRECT(lsa, bdr_router)
 
 // Wait for acknowledgment
 START ack_timer FOR each sent neighbor

RECEIVE_LSA(lsa, from_router):
 // Check if LSA is newer
 IF lsa IS_NEWER_THAN existing_lsa THEN:
 UPDATE lsdb with new lsa
 FLOOD_LSA(lsa, receiving_interface)
 RETURN LSAck
 ELSE:
 // Duplicate or older
 RETURN LSAck (for reliability)

RECEIVE_LSAck(from_router, ack_list):
 FOR each acknowledged_lsa IN ack_list:
 REMOVE acknowledged_lsa FROM retransmission_list
 CANCEL ack_timer
examples.explanation: LSA flooding ensures all routers in an area have identical LSDB. Sequence numbers track LSA versions. LSAs are retransmitted if not acknowledged. Flooding is controlled - on broadcast networks, only DR/BDR flood to reduce redundancy.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) per LSA to flood to n neighbors
examples.spaceComplexity: O(L) where L=total LSAs in LSDB
